skill.name: quality-verify
skill.description: Verify final deliverables against completeness, correctness, consistency, performance, security, and maintainability before delivery.
skill.summary: Run a structured six-dimension quality review before final delivery.
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives from Markdown fences, sample pseudocode, JSON output examples, and ordinary prose. No executable scripts, shell command instructions, weak cryptographic usage, system reconnaissance, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection text were found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings occur in Markdown code fences and examples. They describe scoring formulas, JavaScript-style repository access, JSON output shape, and a decision tree, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are Markdown code blocks and examples, not executable Ruby code. No command invocation or user-controlled shell execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Prose Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear on a skill description line and a documentation checklist line. These lines do not invoke DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context is plain documentation about quality criteria and endpoint completeness. There is no code or cryptographic operation to evaluate.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is General Guidance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding is general quality guidance about standards and consistency. It does not instruct the agent to inspect the host, enumerate files, collect environment data, or run reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is ordinary prose in the notes section. No host inspection, system command, or data collection behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Final reviews often miss gaps in correctness, security, and maintainability. This skill applies a six-dimension checklist and clear scoring before delivery.
actual_capabilities: Reviews deliverables for completeness, correctness, consistency, performance, security, and maintainability.
Scores each applicable quality dimension and calculates an overall readiness score.
Classifies issues as critical, major, or minor for clearer triage.
Provides delivery recommendations based on score thresholds.
Uses project standards when available and general best practices when standards are unclear.
limitations: It provides review guidance and does not execute tests by itself.
It depends on the available deliverable and project standards for accuracy.
It cannot prove factual accuracy without source material or verification tools.
It may require human judgment for subjective quality standards.

use_cases.title: Validate a Code Change
use_cases.description: Check a feature or bug fix against readiness criteria before handing it to a reviewer or user.

use_cases.title: Review Technical Documentation
use_cases.description: Assess documentation for completeness, correctness, consistency, and maintainability before publication.

use_cases.title: Gate Final Client Work
use_cases.description: Apply a structured final review to reduce missing requirements before client delivery.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Final Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after drafting a small deliverable.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quality-verify to review this deliverable. Score each applicable dimension and tell me if it is ready to deliver.

prompt_templates.title: Check Against Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quality-verify on this deliverable against the requirements below. Identify missing items, major risks, and recommended fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Review Code and Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before merging a feature with supporting docs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quality-verify to review the code change and documentation together. Evaluate correctness, security, performance, and maintainability.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Release Gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before a high-impact release or client handoff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quality-verify as a release gate. Apply project standards if available, assign severity to every issue, and recommend deliver, fix, or revise.

output_examples.input: Review a React dropdown component before delivery.
output_examples.output: The component is ready to deliver with a strong overall score. One minor test gap remains for empty input behavior.

output_examples.input: Review API endpoint documentation for publication.
output_examples.output: The documentation is mostly ready. Add missing error response examples before publishing to improve completeness.

output_examples.input: Review a final client report against listed requirements.
output_examples.output: The report needs revision. Two required sections are missing and the recommendations do not match the supplied data.

best_practices: Run the review after the deliverable is complete and before final handoff.
Provide requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria when available.
Resolve critical and major issues before treating the work as ready.
anti_patterns: Do not use the score as a substitute for tests or source verification.
Do not ignore low-scoring dimensions because the overall average is acceptable.
Do not apply generic standards when project-specific standards are available.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews completeness, correctness, consistency, performance, security, and maintainability for the final deliverable.

faq.question: Can it run tests?
faq.answer: No. It gives review guidance and scoring. Separate tools are needed to run automated tests.

faq.question: Does it work for non-code deliverables?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review documentation, reports, and other structured deliverables using applicable quality dimensions.

faq.question: How does it decide readiness?
faq.answer: It scores applicable dimensions, applies issue deductions, and compares the result with readiness thresholds.

faq.question: What should I provide with my prompt?
faq.answer: Provide the deliverable, requirements, project standards, and any known acceptance criteria.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only Markdown instructions and examples, with no confirmed dangerous behavior.
